channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I built a suite of self-destructing web tools on Cloudflare (solo, free tier)
item.description: Most cloud tools are built around saving things. But a lot of the time I don't want to save anything — I just want to hand something off for a few minutes and have it disappear on its own. A mock...

item.title: What I Learned as a Software Testing Intern at Scode Software Solutions
item.description: What I Learned as a Software Testing Intern at Scode Software Solutions As developers, we often focus on building features. But during my internship at Scode Software Solutions, I learned that...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Saving $1000/Month: Our AWS SES Migration Story
item.description: 4:00 PM on a Friday, a ticket came in from our highest-paying customer: "None of our staff are receiving your system alerts." I opened the AWS SES console expecting a quick answer. Instead: no...

item.title: Advanced Server-Side Caching Patterns in Next.js
item.description: Originally published on tamiz.pro. Next.js, with its hybrid rendering capabilities, offers a robust platform for building performant web applications. While client-side caching is well-understood,...

item.title: The AI Era is Here: Your Career in Agentic AI Awaits
item.description: The Moment is Now You've read 10 posts on agentic AI. You understand the concepts. You know the tools. You see the opportunities. Now what? The Skills That Matter in 2026 1. Prompt Engineering...

item.title: Can a Semantic Cache Become Your Primary Retrieval Layer?
item.description: Building a semantic cache layer in front of RAG — and why it might be the most underrated cost optimization in production AI systems. Hey Dev community 👋 Every RAG architecture diagram I see looks...

item.title: How Attackers Think: A Developer's Guide to Seeing Security Through an Attacker's Eyes
item.description: "Security isn't about building walls. It's about understanding how someone will try to climb over them." Most developers build software with one person in mind: the user. Attackers don't. While...

item.title: Building a Kafka-free CDC Pipeline in Go (and the DDL bug that almost broke my brain)
item.description: So I've been obsessed with Postgres internals for a while now - WAL, replication slots, MVCC, all of it. I built pgstream a while back just to read the WAL and stream changes in real time, and it...

item.title: An internet joke made me build this.
item.description: few weeks ago, I noticed something funny. People weren't measuring time in years anymore. They were measuring it in "before GTA VI." "I graduated before GTA VI." "I got married before GTA VI." "I...

item.title: WordPress Isn't Slow. Your Development Is.
item.description: WordPress Isn't Slow. Your Development Is. Performance Starts with Better Development I've been working with WordPress for more than 10 years. One thing I've heard over and over again is:...

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce from the US market after cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market."...

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description: Updated July 18, 2026: the two flaws now carry CVE IDs, the full mechanism has been published, a persistent-object-cache condition has surfaced, and a working proof-of-concept is public. The story...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Washing Up on Cape Cod Beach
item.description: Lots of articles about this. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process...

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: Shark’s versatile ChillPill cooling system is back to its best price
item.description: Portable fans are one of the easiest ways to stay cool during the summer, and you don’t have to spend much to find a decent one. If you’re looking for something more versatile, though, Shark’s...

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
item.description: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being...

item.title: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust
item.description: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short...

item.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack. The malicious package...

item.title: Apple Music is getting a price hike
item.description: Apple Music is more expensive now. In the US, an individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, a $1 bump up from the previous $10.99 price. A family plan now costs $19.99 per month, up from $16.99,...

item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware...

item.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
item.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does...

item.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
item.description: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with...

item.title: Asus’ top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off
item.description: The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond...

item.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event,...
